content:
# Deuteronomy Chapter 7 — SOPHIA Consensus Translation
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **Sources:** EMET (literal), LOGOS (formal), PNEUMA (dynamic) | **Leningrad Codex:** Folios 204–205 | **Vaticanus:** pp. 148–149

---

## Council Notes

**Chapter Overview:** Chapter 7 opens the second major section of Moses' address, moving from historical remembrance to practical command. The chapter covers: the mandate for total destruction (cherem) of Canaanite nations (vv.1–5); Israel's election as a holy, treasured people (vv.6–8); the faithfulness of the covenant God (vv.9–11); promises of blessing for obedience (vv.12–16); and exhortations against fear with reminders of divine power (vv.17–26). The chapter ends with the cherem bracket begun in v.2.

**Key Rulings:**
- **cherem (vv.2,26):** All three agents render "utterly devote to destruction" / "devoted to destruction." SOPHIA rules: retain "utterly devote them to destruction" (v.2) and "devoted to destruction" (v.26) — preserving the covenantal-legal force of the Hebrew.
- **am segullah (v.6):** EMET: "treasured people"; LOGOS: "people for his treasured possession"; PNEUMA: "his own treasured possession." SOPHIA rules: "a people of his own treasured possession" — capturing both the relational and ownership dimensions.
- **hesed (vv.9,12):** All three render "covenant-faithfulness" per established convention. Retained.
- **tsir'ah (v.20):** All three render "hornet(s)." Retained.
- **to'evah (vv.25,26):** EMET/LOGOS: "abomination"; PNEUMA: "detestable." SOPHIA rules: "abomination" — matching the established convention for to'evah.
- **masseboth (v.5):** EMET: "pillars [masseboth]"; LOGOS: "pillars"; PNEUMA: "sacred pillars." Consensus: "pillars" — no gloss needed at every occurrence.
- **"more numerous" vs. "greater" (v.1):** EMET/LOGOS: "greater and more numerous"; PNEUMA: "larger and more powerful." Consensus: "greater and more numerous" — more literal to Hebrew rabbim u-atsumim.
- **v.2 structure:** EMET keeps cherem formula as one sentence; LOGOS breaks it; PNEUMA introduces "ban of destruction." Consensus: follows EMET/LOGOS pattern.
- **v.4:** EMET: "your son"; LOGOS: "your sons"; PNEUMA: "your children." The Hebrew singular (binkha) is unusual; LOGOS normalizes to plural. Consensus: "your son" — preserving the MT singular.
- **v.10:** EMET: "to their face, to destroy them"; LOGOS: "by destroying them"; PNEUMA: "he repays with destruction." Consensus: follows EMET's more literal rendering.
- **v.14:** EMET: "a barren male or a barren female"; LOGOS: "male or female barren"; PNEUMA: "a man or woman among you will be barren." Consensus: "a barren male or a barren female" — literal to 'aqar and 'aqarah.
- **v.20:** EMET: "until those who are left and who hide themselves perish"; LOGOS: "until those who are left and hide themselves from you are destroyed"; PNEUMA: "until even the survivors who have hidden themselves from you are destroyed." Consensus: "until those who are left and hide themselves are destroyed."
- **v.22:** EMET: "lest the wild animals become too numerous against you"; LOGOS: "lest the wild beasts grow too numerous for you"; PNEUMA: "or the wild animals will multiply against you." Consensus: "lest the wild beasts grow too numerous against you."
- **v.26 double verb:** EMET: "you shall utterly loathe it and utterly abhor it"; LOGOS: "You shall utterly detest and abhor it"; PNEUMA: "Treat it as utterly revolting and repulsive." Consensus: "you shall utterly detest it and utterly abhor it" — preserving the double Hiphil construction.

---

## Consensus Translation — Deuteronomy 7

**1** When the LORD your God brings you into the land you are going in to possess, and he drives out many nations from before you — the Hittites and the Girgashites and the Amorites and the Canaanites and the Perizzites and the Hivites and the Jebusites — seven nations greater and more numerous than you,

**2** and the LORD your God gives them over before you, and you strike them down — you shall utterly devote them to destruction. You shall make no covenant with them and show them no favor.

**3** You shall not intermarry with them; your daughter you shall not give to his son, and his daughter you shall not take for your son,

**4** for he would turn your son away from following me, and they would serve other gods; then the anger of the LORD would be kindled against you and he would destroy you quickly.

**5** But thus shall you deal with them: their altars you shall tear down, and their pillars you shall smash, and their Asherah poles you shall hew down, and their carved images you shall burn with fire.

**6** For you are a holy people to the LORD your God; the LORD your God has chosen you to be a people of his own treasured possession out of all the peoples who are on the face of the earth.

**7** Not because you were more numerous than all the peoples did the LORD set his love on you and choose you — for you were the fewest of all peoples —

**8** but because of the LORD's love for you and his keeping of the oath that he swore to your fathers, the LORD brought you out with a strong hand and redeemed you from the house of slavery, from the hand of Pharaoh king of Egypt.

**9** Know therefore that the LORD your God, he is God, the faithful God, keeping the covenant and the covenant-faithfulness with those who love him and keep his commandments, to a thousand generations,

**10** and repaying to their face those who hate him, by destroying them. He will not delay with the one who hates him — to his face he will repay him.

**11** And you shall keep the commandment and the statutes and the ordinances that I am commanding you today, to do them.

**12** And because you heed these ordinances and keep and do them, the LORD your God will keep with you the covenant and the covenant-faithfulness that he swore to your fathers.

**13** He will love you and bless you and multiply you, and he will bless the fruit of your womb and the fruit of your ground, your grain and your new wine and your fresh oil, the offspring of your cattle and the young of your flock, in the land that he swore to your fathers to give you.

**14** You shall be blessed above all peoples; there shall not be a barren male or a barren female among you, or among your livestock.

**15** And the LORD will turn away from you every sickness; and all the evil diseases of Egypt, which you knew, he will not put upon you, but he will lay them on all who hate you.

**16** And you shall consume all the peoples that the LORD your God gives over to you; your eye shall not look with pity on them; and you shall not serve their gods, for that would be a snare to you.

**17** If you say in your heart, "These nations are more numerous than I — how am I able to dispossess them?" —

**18** you shall not be afraid of them; you shall well remember what the LORD your God did to Pharaoh and to all Egypt:

**19** the great trials that your eyes saw, and the signs and the wonders and the strong hand and the outstretched arm by which the LORD your God brought you out — so shall the LORD your God do to all the peoples of whom you are afraid.

**20** And also the hornets the LORD your God will send among them, until those who are left and hide themselves are destroyed before you.

**21** You shall not tremble before them, for the LORD your God is in your midst, a great and awesome God.

**22** And the LORD your God will clear away these nations before you little by little; you may not make an end of them all at once, lest the wild beasts grow too numerous against you.

**23** And the LORD your God will give them over before you and will throw them into great confusion until they are destroyed.

**24** And he will give their kings into your hand, and you shall make their name perish from under the heavens; no man shall stand before you until you have destroyed them.

**25** The carved images of their gods you shall burn with fire; you shall not covet the silver or the gold that is on them or take it for yourself, lest you be ensnared by it, for it is an abomination to the LORD your God.

**26** And you shall not bring an abomination into your house and become devoted to destruction like it; you shall utterly detest it and utterly abhor it, for it is devoted to destruction.

---

## Verse-by-Verse Ruling Notes

**V.1:** All three agents agree on the seven-nation list and basic structure. PNEUMA uses "larger and more powerful" — overruled in favor of EMET/LOGOS "greater and more numerous" (literal to rabbim u-atsumim).

**V.2:** EMET uses "utterly devote them to destruction [cherem]" with bracketed gloss. LOGOS same without gloss. PNEUMA uses "place them under the ban of destruction." Ruling: retain EMET/LOGOS phrasing; gloss dropped as cherem is now established convention in this translation.

**V.4:** Hebrew binkha is singular. EMET preserves singular "your son"; LOGOS normalizes to "your sons." Ruling: preserve MT singular "your son."

**V.6:** Three-way divergence on am segullah rendering. Ruling: "a people of his own treasured possession" — synthesizing LOGOS's formal structure with PNEUMA's relational "own."

**V.9:** PNEUMA renders "covenant of love" (combining berit and hesed). Ruling: keep hesed separate as "covenant-faithfulness" per established convention; do not blend the terms.

**V.10:** EMET preserves "to their face" (twice) matching Hebrew panav. LOGOS drops second instance. Ruling: keep both, matching MT structure.

**V.14:** "barren male or barren female" — EMET's literal rendering preserved.

**V.20:** PNEUMA adds "even" for rhetorical emphasis. Consensus smooths without adding unwarranted emphasis.

**V.22:** "wild beasts" (LOGOS) preferred to "wild animals" (EMET/PNEUMA) — consistent with formal register.

**V.25–26:** "abomination" retained for to'evah throughout. PNEUMA's "detestable" set aside.

**V.26:** Hebrew double Hiphil infinitive absolute construction (shaqets teshaqtsenu, ta'ev teta'avenu) preserved with "utterly detest...utterly abhor."

---

## Textual Notes

**V.1 — Seven nations:** All three source traditions (LC, LXX Vaticanus pp.148–149) preserve the same seven-nation list. The Girgashites appear in both MT and LXX; no textual dispute.

**V.2 — cherem:** Hebrew חֵרֶם — total devotion to destruction, the holy-war ban. LXX: ἀναθεματιεῖτε (anathematize/put under the ban). The double absolute-infinitive construction in Hebrew (hacharem tacharimu) signals the absolute nature of the command.

**V.6 — am segullah:** LXX: λαὸν περιούσιον (a people of special possession) — supports the ownership nuance in "treasured possession."

**V.8 — hesed:** LXX: ἐλέους (mercy). The Hebrew hesed encompasses covenantal fidelity beyond mere mercy; "covenant-faithfulness" is retained per project convention.

**V.9 — "to a thousand generations":** MT singular דּוֹר (dor, generation); LXX pluralizes to γενεάς. MT preserved.

**V.20 — tsir'ah:** LXX: σφηκίαν (wasp-nest). The literal zoological reading is preserved. Allegorical interpretations (panic, disease) are noted but set aside.

**V.25 — to'evah:** LXX: βδέλυγμα (detestable thing). "Abomination" retained as established convention.

**V.26 — double cherem:** The chapter opens and closes with the cherem theme (vv.2 and 26), forming a deliberate bracket. The infection-quality of the ban — that an Israelite bringing cherem into the house becomes cherem — is preserved in the translation structure.

— SOPHIA
